subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #4 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Apple Day
Lots of Fun
As a school community, we enjoyed a morning of apple-inspired activities to cap off the first four weeks of school!!
Back blacktop area:
Children measured and weighed apples, experimented with balancing scales, explored a sensory box filled with apple-themed items, and created with lots of playdough.
Front steps area:
Ms. Jenny led us in
Apple Tree Yoga
and the children made festive apple headbands.
Garden area:
Friends played “apple roll” with red balls and worked hard to keep apple balloons from falling.
Playground area:
We wrapped up with an apple spoon race and a fun “apple-hole” tossing game.
It was a day filled with laughter, bravery in trying new things, and kindness toward friends. Thank you for sharing these wonderful children with us—they made the day truly special!
Gardening with Ms. Linda
It’s officially Fall as we celebrate the changing of the seasons by looking carefully at how our garden has transformed in the past few weeks. Our herb garden was beginning to be overrun by grass and weeds. Their roots are strong and must be loosened by digging deep with a shovel before pulling them out. We learned to remove dried stalks by pressing down and then pulling them out. We cut back the tomato plants including one plant that germinated in the compost bin and pushed its way out a hole into the sunshine. We are clearing the beds of dried plant materials before we add a layer of compost and topsoil to prepare for winter. All this “green” material went into the compost bin as an important source of nitrogen. Next, we added water and aerated the pile with a pitchfork. Next week, we will add a layer of dried leaves (as a source of carbon) and 
completed compost to feed the millions of “decomposers” that do the work of breaking down organic matter and turning it into soil or “black gold”.
Our Kindergarten children picked all the remaining cucumbers before pulling out the dried plants. To preserve the last of the harvest they made half-sour pickles using a salt brine, garlic, spices and herbs.
Tuesday - PEP Spirit
Pizza Cupcake Lunch
Wear your school t-shirt
or any blue shirt
On
Tuesday, September 30th
, we will celebrate all September birthdays with a pizza and cupcake lunch. Children with allergies will be accommodated—I’ll reach out to confirm pizza shop and brand details with you directly.
For families who order lunch through
Simply Gourmet
, please remember that
Wednesday begins a new month
, so you’ll need to place an order to cover the mid-week 
start.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny introduced the children to one of my favorite art techniques - block printing.  The results are stunning.
Upcoming
Tuesday, Sept. 30th - PEP Pizza Cupcake Day (wear school t-shirt)
Monday, October 13th - Indigenous People's Day - SOCDS Closed
Week of October 13th - MEND Food Drive - non perishable food goods and personal items

SimplyGourmet Lunch Link

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
